game_info.title: Spill It! - Physics Puzzle Game
game_info.concept: **Spill It!** Players engage in a physics-based puzzle by tapping anywhere on the top half of the screen to drop a limited number of balls, typically 3-5 per level, from a fixed or player-selected horizontal position. The dropped balls fall under gravity, interacting with various environmental elements and the primary targets: glasses, which are designed to be easily toppled. The core objective for each level is to knock over a specific number of glasses, usually 80-100% of those present, using the allotted balls, with a level failing if the target count is not met. Successfully completing levels progresses the player through a linear series of increasingly complex puzzles, introducing new obstacles and more intricate glass arrangements. Accumulating successful level completions unlocks new cosmetic ball skins and visual themes for the game environment, enhancing customization. Difficulty scales by increasing the number of glasses, introducing protective barriers, and demanding more precise ball drops to achieve the objective.
game_info.description: Drop balls to topple glasses in this physics-based puzzle game! Each level presents a set of glasses that you must knock over by strategically dropping a limited number of balls. You need to knock over at least 80% of the glasses to progress to the next level. Complete levels to unlock new ball skins and themes. The challenge increases with more glasses, obstacles, and trickier arrangements!
game_info.controls:
LEFT/RIGHT ARROW or A/D: Move ball drop position horizontally
SPACE: Drop a ball from current position
ENTER: Start game from title screen
ESC: Pause/Unpause during gameplay
R: Restart to title screen from game over

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic ball dropping mechanics and physics interactions. This test verifies that balls spawn correctly, fall under gravity, and interact with the environment including glasses and boundaries.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Automatically drop all available balls at different horizontal positions across the screen width. Balls should be dropped at evenly spaced intervals to cover the full drop zone area.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: All balls should spawn at the specified positions, fall downward due to gravity, bounce realistically on collision with surfaces, and interact properly with glasses causing them to tip over. The test is successful if balls behave according to physics without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by ensuring enough glasses are knocked over. This verifies the level completion logic and progression system works correctly when the 80% threshold is met.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Drop balls at optimal positions calculated to maximize glass knockdown. Target the center of glass clusters and areas where chain reactions can occur to topple multiple glasses with each ball.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The game should detect when 80% or more glasses are knocked over, transition to the win state, display victory message with level completion stats, and allow progression to the next level. Test succeeds when win condition triggers correctly.
